description_long: This is a medium-sized, polygonal wooden bucket with a slightly tapered cylindrical shape and a wide open top. The exterior is constructed from reddish-brown wooden staves, each with visible wood grain and stylized chisel marks, giving it a hand-crafted, rustic appearance. Two thick, metallic gray bands encircle the bucket—one near the top and one near the bottom—serving as structural reinforcement. A robust, angular metal bail handle is attached to the upper band, designed for easy carrying and tilting. The interior is hollow and matches the exterior wood texture, with no visible lining or coating. The bucket is utilitarian and sturdy, suitable for carrying water, tools, or other materials in an urban or outdoor setting.
description: A polygonal wooden bucket with metal bands and a sturdy metal handle, featuring a rustic, hand-crafted look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, both metal bands, and the handle resting across the front.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angular handle attachment and the wood grain details on the staves.
description_view_2: Rear view with the handle mostly hidden, emphasizing the uninterrupted wood and metal banding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the handle angled outward, showing the attachment point and banding.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
